title: NGEs TV Ending Budget
wikitext:
[]: NGE's TV Ending Budget             Quote Post by Rabid-Sachiel » Tue Feb 16, 2010 12:37 pm We've all heard rumors that Anno made the last 2 eps of NGE the way he did because of budget issues. Is this really true or did he choose to make the eps be more abstract intentionally?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Feb 16, 2010 12:44 pm As far as I know, it wasn't just budget issues but some combination of budget, time limitations, and network pressure. The first five or six episodes of the series were produced before it started airing, I believe, and were shipped around to networks to look for a network to buy it. So, when the show first started airing they had a relatively large time buffer for producing new episodes, but as the show went on they got behind schedule enough that they had to do the last couple of episodes more or less entirely within the week before they were broadcast. This resulted in a lot of late nights, unpaid overtime, outsourcing, reuse of already-finished work from previous episodes, and abstraction to enable the final episodes to be completed within the extremely tight production time and budget constraints. The gradual turn towards abstraction in the second half of the series was almost certainly also an intentional creative decision, but I'd imagine that if the last two episodes were made that way solely because they wanted them to be (i.e. if they weren't originally supposed to be a shorter, somewhat tamer version of EoE), we would have seen a lot more new footage in them, scenes wouldn't abruptly switch from finished animation to marker sketches, etc. I can't really think of any reason why the instrumentality scenes wouldn't have worked just as well or better using new animation for the characters in them (see: some of EoE's preinstrumentality/instrumentality stuff), rather than recycled scenes with new backdrops and dialogue. Anyone feel free to correct me if I've gotten any facts wrong.

[]: Re: NGE's TV Ending Budget             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Feb 16, 2010 12:54 pm Rabid-Sachiel wrote: We've all heard rumors that Anno made the last 2 eps of NGE the way he did because of budget issues. Is this really true or did he choose to make the eps be more abstract intentionally?

[]:             Quote Post by Rabid-Sachiel » Tue Feb 16, 2010 2:04 pm Shin-seiki wrote: But it's also apparent that Anno may well have been aiming to do something like D&R/EoE all along, (originally as an OVA, but soon it became a movie project), bcause evidence is that work was going forward on it almost immediately after the original airing wrapped up.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Tue Feb 16, 2010 3:56 pm I doubt the movie was intentionally made to be like Ideon's way of telling the story. TV Series with a messed up ending then Movie for a proper ending/Alternate Ending. Also, I heard Eva and Ideon had more in common than just the ending, a fair few things, maybe i'm wrong

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Feb 16, 2010 4:15 pm Rabid-Sachiel wrote: Also, another theory is: Anno made EoE because people sent angry letters, and he sought to improve the ending. Or he wasn't completely satisfied with the last 2 eps and sought to improve it by making it even more deep.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Feb 16, 2010 4:56 pm esselfortium wrote: I think this theory is at least mostly debunked when you consider that the majority of the fan-letters that are flashed onscreen during EoE were praising the series' ending. And I'm pretty sure at least some of the hate mail ("I'll kill you, Anno! I'll kill you!") that was shown along with it was in response to Death & Rebirth's cliffhanger ending, not to the TV ending. ..I think so, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Feb 16, 2010 5:48 pm Anno made EoE because people sent angry letters, and he sought to improve the ending. Or he wasn't completely satisfied with the last 2 eps and sought to improve it by making it even more deep.

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Tue Feb 16, 2010 5:55 pm Okay. I only have NGE as a bunch of avi files. I know, I know... But what's the deal with that weird, sketched-out preview at the end of 24 that shows mainly Asuka battling the MP Evas? Was that actually aired with the original broadcast or just put in there for fun by the dude-that-made-the-NGE-torrent ? Cause if it was aired in the original broadcast... why? It seems like they had 25' already planned out and even did a "test" but if 25 was already supposed to be the way it was, why make it look differently? I don't even have a "normal" preview for 25. I just have the unfinished one that was supposed to be for "Air."

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Feb 16, 2010 6:07 pm That preview was only in the director's cut version of 24 (24'); the original aired version didn't have it. The directors' cut episodes were made to go along with EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Tue Feb 16, 2010 6:27 pm Oh. Good to know, thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Feb 16, 2010 10:29 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: and lets not count out the multitude of yaoi fanboys and fangirls who were pissed at the irrefudable proof that Shinji was straight , even though he was clearly fucked up in the head .

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 17, 2010 1:56 am chaosakita wrote: lowut?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Wed Feb 17, 2010 10:25 pm Rabid Sachiel wrote: Also, another theory is: Anno made EoE because people sent angry letters, and he sought to improve the ending. Or he wasn't completely satisfied with the last 2 eps and sought to improve it by making it even more deep.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 17, 2010 11:18 pm planet news wrote: Cause if it was aired in the original broadcast... why? It seems like they had 25' already planned out and even did a "test" but if 25 was already supposed to be the way it was, why make it look differently

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Feb 18, 2010 4:02 pm If episodes 18 and 20 never occurred or were altered, this budget problem wouldn't happen. Yet again... if they did that, there might not be an EoE or the success and popularity of the show. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Feb 18, 2010 9:31 pm tomrule123 wrote: If episodes 18 and 20 never occurred or were altered, this budget problem wouldn't happen. Yet again... if they did that, there might not be an EoE or the success and popularity of the show. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Feb 18, 2010 11:49 pm esselfortium wrote: tomrule123 wrote: If episodes 18 and 20 never occurred or were altered, this budget problem wouldn't happen. Yet again... if they did that, there might not be an EoE or the success and popularity of the show. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Fri Feb 19, 2010 5:32 pm Ornette wrote: So at least they have to have had an idea of what they were going to do. At this point they either didn't have it finished (very likely) or just wanted to have an "unfinished" look for the preview. TV productions are usually an episode or 2 ahead of what just got aired on TV, if they didn't even have the episode done, that gives them a little over a week to make episode 25 and get it to the broadcasters. The thing that doesn't get mentioned enough is that they really didn't have any time to work on the last 2 episodes. Given what the staff says about the production of the last 2 episodes, they could have given up and just have the show end at 24 episodes then finish it up with an OVA or movie, or they could at least try to do something. The entirety of Gainax probably got little to no sleep for those 2 weeks, and with no money to get outsourced help, that was all that could be managed

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Thu Oct 07, 2010 2:02 pm Ornette wrote: From 11/96 Newtype in Protoculture Addicts #43
